title: Word counter
shortDescription: Count words, characters, paragraphs, and estimated reading time from any text.
metaDescription: Count words, characters, paragraphs, and estimated reading time from pasted text.
intro: Paste text and get the word count, character count, paragraph count, and estimated reading time immediately.
overview:
This word counter is built for text that needs a precise length check before it is published, submitted, or shared. It handles pasted copy, drafts, notes, and structured paragraphs without changing your text.

Use it when you need to compare versions of a paragraph, verify a headline draft, or confirm whether a block fits a limit. The reading-time estimate is based on a standard 200 words per minute, so it gives a practical approximation for articles and longer notes.
howItWorks: Paste or type text into the input area.
The tool counts words, characters, and paragraph blocks from the text you provide.
Reading time is estimated from the total word count using a 200 words-per-minute rate.

useCases.title: Headline and subheading drafts
useCases.description: Check whether a title, subtitle, or section header stays within a target length before you finalize the layout.

useCases.title: Article length review
useCases.description: Measure draft articles, introductions, and body sections to keep each part aligned with an editorial brief.

useCases.title: Client copy approval
useCases.description: Confirm that landing page copy, ad text, or product descriptions fits the space agreed with a client or designer.

useCases.title: Assignment and note limits
useCases.description: Review essays, summaries, and study notes against required word or character limits before submission.

examples.title: Landing page intro
examples.input: Build trust with clear pricing, fast support, and no hidden fees.
examples.description: Useful when a short block must stay tight and readable.

examples.title: Two-paragraph blog draft
examples.input:
Design systems improve consistency across product teams.
They also reduce rework during handoff and review.

examples.title: Meeting note excerpt
examples.input: Confirm venue, send agenda, collect slides, and share the recap by Friday.
examples.description: Handy for short internal notes where length still matters.

faq.question: How are words counted in this tool?
faq.answer: Words are counted by separating text into readable word units after extra spaces are ignored. Numbers and mixed text are counted as tokens only when they are separated like normal words.

faq.question: What counts as a paragraph?
faq.answer: Paragraphs are counted as separate blocks divided by blank lines. A single line break inside the same block does not create a new paragraph.

faq.question: Why does the reading time look approximate?
faq.answer: The estimate uses a fixed pace of 200 words per minute. Dense technical text, bullets, or short notes may feel different from normal prose.

faq.question: Can punctuation change the result?
faq.answer: Punctuation does not add extra words by itself. However, unusual spacing, pasted lists, or line breaks can affect how the text is split into units.

faq.question: Why might the count differ from a document editor?
faq.answer: Different editors use different counting rules for hyphenated terms, emojis, URLs, and headings. This tool follows its own text-splitting rules, so results can vary slightly.

seo.title: Word Counter Online
seo.description: Count words, characters, paragraphs, and reading time from text with a clean, focused word counter.
